bugs.root_cause: NVIDIA gpu-operator Helm chart v25.10.1 renders the upgrade-crd Helm pre-upgrade hook's ServiceAccount in namespace 'nvidia-gpu-operator' (hardcoded template), but the Job pod and ClusterRoleBinding subject reference the SA in Release.Namespace='gpu-operator'. Job cannot start pod → ArgoCD stalls in PreSync phase indefinitely → CRDs never install → operator never deploys.
bugs.fix: Add a persistent SA with the same name in gpu-operator namespace as a PreSync hook (argocd.argoproj.io/hook: PreSync, sync-wave: -1) so it applies BEFORE the chart's hook Job (wave 0). Also correct hostPaths.driverInstallDir from /usr/local/glibc/usr to /usr/local/glibc/usr/lib per Talos 1.13 NVIDIA Proprietary Drivers guide.

bugs.error_message: readiness check failed: kopia repository status failed: signal: killed
bugs.root_cause: readinessProbe httpGet defaults to timeoutSeconds:1 when unset. /readyz handler runs kopia subprocess which takes >1s over NFS with 24 snapshot sources. Kubelet's 1s HTTP timeout cancels the request context, Go exec.CommandContext sends SIGKILL to kopia subprocess. Architectural: handler runs kopia on every probe — expensive.
bugs.fix: Set timeoutSeconds:15 on readinessProbe, initialDelaySeconds:60 (first NFS kopia connect takes ~57s), periodSeconds:30, failureThreshold:3. Liveness: timeoutSeconds:5, initialDelaySeconds:60. Follow-up: simplify HealthCheck() in internal/kopia/client.go to os.Stat(repoPath) + c.connected — avoid kopia subprocess on probe path.

bugs.error_message: Wrong reference: b8665 should be b8783
NVIDIA pods won't deploy / nvidia-gpu-operator namespace appears empty
bugs.root_cause: The infrastructure ApplicationSet derives destination.namespace from path.basename (`nvidia-gpu-operator`), but the GPU operator manifests explicitly deploy to `gpu-operator`. ArgoCD auto-creates an empty `nvidia-gpu-operator` namespace while the real NVIDIA workloads run in `gpu-operator`, which makes namespace-scoped checks look like a deployment failure and leaves the app OutOfSync.
bugs.fix: No repo change in this session. Verified that live workloads are healthy in `gpu-operator`; future fix should align the ApplicationSet destination namespace with the manifests or add a special-case override for this app.

bugs.error_message: karakeep.vanillax.me (and potentially other *.vanillax.me hostnames) returning HTTP 403 "Access denied" from server: cloudflare edge, while in-cluster curl to karakeep-web:3000 returns healthy 307
bugs.root_cause: cloudflared pods got into a bad state. Two compounding issues: (1) cloudflared aggressively caches origin DNS resolution for cilium-gateway-gateway-external.gateway.svc.cluster.local and does not re-resolve when gateway pods reschedule; (2) long-lived QUIC tunnel connections (protocol: quic) can wedge — cloudflared pod shows Running but Cloudflare edge treats the tunnel session as unhealthy and returns 403 for routed requests. With 3 replicas, failures are intermittent (~33% per bad replica).
bugs.fix: Delete cloudflared pods — fresh pods re-resolve service DNS and establish new QUIC sessions. Long-term: add livenessProbe hitting http://localhost:2000/ready on the cloudflared container so K8s auto-restarts wedged pods. Consider switching protocol: quic → http2 if recurrent. Periodic CronJob restart is a crude backstop.

bugs.error_message: Wrong reference: quic should be http2
llama-cpp pod Pending: 0/7 nodes are available: 1 Insufficient cpu
bugs.root_cause: GPU worker node was scaled down to 8 vCPU (~7950m allocatable), but llama-cpp requested 8 CPU cores. With ~7701m already requested on that node, scheduler could never place the pod.
bugs.fix: Reduced llama-cpp CPU request from 8 to 2 while keeping GPU=1 and existing limits so the pod can schedule on the scaled-down GPU node.

bugs.error_message: llama-cpp pod Pending after reboot: Insufficient nvidia.com/gpu and Insufficient cpu
bugs.root_cause: After GPU worker VM reboot, NVIDIA daemonset pods on the node were in Unknown state and node advertised nvidia.com/gpu allocatable=0. Scheduler could not place llama-cpp until GPU resources were re-registered.
bugs.fix: Restarted gpu-operator daemonsets (nvidia-device-plugin-daemonset, gpu-feature-discovery, nvidia-operator-validator) to refresh node GPU resource registration. Node returned to nvidia.com/gpu capacity/allocatable=8 and llama-cpp scheduled/ran with cpu request 6.

ImagePullBackOff: Back-off pulling image "bitnami/kubectl:1.33.1" on cloudflared-restart-29610960-jzd2f (4522 attempts over 17h)
bugs.root_cause: Bitnami moved kubectl image to 'bitnamilegacy' org; bitnami/kubectl:1.33.1 no longer exists. Commit 7761c9d7 fixed the CronJob spec to use bitnamilegacy/kubectl:1.33.1, but the previously-created Job 'cloudflared-restart-29610960' was already running with the old image and K8s Jobs are immutable — the spec update did not propagate to the running Job. Kubelet retried the bad pull indefinitely.
bugs.fix: kubectl -n cloudflared delete job cloudflared-restart-29610960 so the next CronJob firing creates a fresh Job from the corrected spec. Verified by manually triggering: kubectl -n cloudflared create job --from=cronjob/cloudflared-restart test — pod pulled bitnamilegacy/kubectl:1.33.1 and completed in ~20s with 'deployment.apps/cloudflared restarted'.
